task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Semiconductors' watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a tab, dropdown menu item, or window title in the JStock application clearly labeled 'Semiconductors'.

task_completion.description: Verify NVDA is in the 'Semiconductors' watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Semiconductors' watchlist is active or visible, look for the stock symbol 'NVDA' listed in the main data table.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Software & Cloud' watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a tab, dropdown menu item, or window title in the JStock application clearly labeled 'Software & Cloud'.

task_completion.description: Verify MSFT and GOOGL are in the 'Software & Cloud' watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Software & Cloud' watchlist is active or visible, look for the stock symbols 'MSFT' and 'GOOGL' listed in the main data table.

task_completion.description: Verify 'My Watchlist' contains only AAPL and AMZN, and excludes the moved stocks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When 'My Watchlist' is active, look for 'AAPL' and 'AMZN' in the data table. Ensure that 'NVDA', 'MSFT', and 'GOOGL' are no longer visible in this specific watchlist.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the JStock graphical user interface to manage watchlists.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the JStock application open with active menus, dialog boxes, or right-click context menus being used, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the watchlists and stock symbols are displayed within the actual JStock application interface (tables, tabs) and not inside a text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing application configuration files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the absence of terminal windows showing commands like 'sed', 'echo', or 'nano' being used to modify JStock's underlying XML or CSV files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target watchlists and their exact contents: 'My Watchlist' must contain exactly ['AAPL', 'AMZN']. 'Semiconductors' must contain exactly ['NVDA']. 'Software & Cloud' must contain exactly ['MSFT', 'GOOGL']. The stocks ['NVDA', 'MSFT', 'GOOGL'] are forbidden in 'My Watchlist' and must be removed from it.
